# The tricky case: voting while engaged. The witness must not grant a vote
# that could allow a candidate to become leader without the entries the
# witness helped commit.

# Set up: engaged in term a at a5.
set
term=a voted-for=v1 hi=a5 engaged=true
----
w:  vote=(a, v1) acked=(a5, engaged)

# Candidate at term b with log ending at a5: rejected.
# The candidate's last log term (a) is not ahead of our vote term (a), so
# we can't prove a proper election happened without us. The old leader may
# have relied on us to commit entries beyond a5.
vote from=v2 term=b lm=a5
----
rejected

# Candidate at term b with log ending at a100: still rejected.
# Even though the candidate has more entries, they're all from term a.
# The old leader could have committed with our help up to any index.
vote from=v2 term=b lm=a100
----
rejected

# Candidate at term b with log ending at b1: accepted!
# The candidate has an entry from term b > our vote term a. This proves
# a proper leader election happened for some term > a without the witness,
# meaning a traditional quorum elected that leader, and the candidate's
# log is up to date with anything committed.
vote from=v2 term=b lm=b1
----
ok
w:  vote=(b, v2) acked=(a∞, released)

# Same scenario but candidate is from a much higher term.
set
term=a voted-for=v1 hi=a5 engaged=true
----
w:  vote=(a, v1) acked=(a5, engaged)

# Candidate from term e with log at c3: accepted.
# Log term c > vote term a proves disengagement is safe.
vote from=v3 term=e lm=c3
----
ok
w:  vote=(e, v3) acked=(a∞, released)

# Engagement was released (not engaged), candidate log behind hi: rejected
# on log-up-to-date check, not engagement.
set
term=a voted-for=v1 hi=a5 engaged=false
----
w:  vote=(a, v1) acked=(a5, released)

vote from=v2 term=b lm=a4
----
rejected

# Same but candidate log at a5: accepted (log is up to date with hi).
vote from=v2 term=b lm=a5
----
ok
w:  vote=(b, v2) acked=(a5, released)

# Witness learns of term b through an engage, then the leader requests a
# vote in that same term. The witness hasn't voted for anyone in term b yet,
# so this should succeed.
set
term=a voted-for=v1
----
w:  vote=(a, v1) acked=none

engage from=v1 term=b lm=b1
----
ok
w:  vote=(b, v1) acked=(b1, engaged)

# Leader now asks for our vote in term b. We haven't voted yet in this term,
# so this is granted, preserving the existing acked state.
vote from=v1 term=b lm=b1
----
ok
w:  vote=(b, v1) acked=(b1, engaged)

# Engage populates VotedFor when it was previously unset. Even when the
# witness learned about the term through some other path (e.g. maybeBumpTerm
# from a different message), an engage from a leader records the leader's
# identity.
set
term=b
----
w:  vote=(b, -) acked=none

engage from=v1 term=b lm=b5
----
ok
w:  vote=(b, v1) acked=(b5, engaged)

# Engaged leader re-campaigns at a higher term. Even though the witness is
# still engaged and the candidate's last log term (b) is not strictly ahead
# of the engagement term (b), the candidate IS the leader we supported, so
# the witness grants the vote. The leader's log is by definition at least
# as up-to-date as what the witness helped commit.
# Note that acked becomes b∞: the witness is no longer engaged, but preserves
# a high water mark reflecting that it may have helped commit anything in term b.
# It has slightly better information here - we just learned the old leader's
# actual last log index. We don't exploit this case since there's no benefit
# and the vote code would be more complex; a new engagement can be granted
# for term c only anyway at this point.
vote from=v1 term=c lm=b5
----
ok
w:  vote=(c, v1) acked=(b∞, released)

# Contrast: a *different* candidate at the same term is still rejected.
set
term=b voted-for=v1 hi=b5 engaged=true
----
w:  vote=(b, v1) acked=(b5, engaged)

vote from=v2 term=c lm=b5
----
rejected

# Engagement unwound by a competing candidate whose log proves a post-engagement
# election (log term c > engagement term b). The witness votes for v2, and the
# engagement winds down to b∞.
set
term=b voted-for=v1 hi=b5 engaged=true
----
w:  vote=(b, v1) acked=(b5, engaged)

vote from=v2 term=c lm=c3
----
ok
w:  vote=(c, v2) acked=(b∞, released)

# The old leader tries to campaign. Its log is still at term b, which can't
# satisfy b∞. Without prevote, this is a real availability loss — the old leader
# can't win despite its log being authoritative for term b, which it would have
# been able to had there not be the failed campaign attempt by the other candidate
# which effectively disabled the "exception" we have for re-electing the old
# leader. This could be improved, but pre-vote exists, so not worth it.
vote from=v1 term=d lm=b10
----
rejected

# A candidate with entries from term > b can satisfy b∞. The b∞ high water
# mark is carried forward — Hi is monotonically non-decreasing. This is safe
# because any future engagement must be at [newTerm, X] or later, which is
# strictly up to date with any older promises we made.
vote from=v1 term=d lm=c1
----
ok
w:  vote=(d, v1) acked=(b∞, released)
